div.p: Without attempting to describe this mysterious connection, and the laws that govern the body and the spirit of man,1 their relationship to each other, and the design of God2 in relation to the human body and spirit, I would just remark, that the spirits of men are eternal,3 that they are governed by the same Priesthood that Abraham, Melchizedek, and the Apostles were: that they are organized4 according to that Priesthood which is everlasting,5 “without beginning of days or end of years,”6—that they all move in their respective spheres, and are governed by the law of God;7 that when they appear upon the earth they are in a probationary state,8 and are preparing, if righteous, for a future and greater glory; that the spirits of good men cannot interfere with the wicked beyond their prescribed bounds, for Michael, the Archangel, dared not bring a railing accusation against the devil, but said, “The Lord rebuke thee, Satan.”9

It would seem also, that wicked spirits have their bounds, limits, and laws by which they are governed or controlled, and know their future destiny; hence, those that were in the maniac said to our Savior, “Art thou come to torment us before the time,”10 and when Satan presented himself before the Lord, among the sons of God, he said that he came “from going to and fro in the earth, and from wandering up and down in it;”11 and he is emphatically called the prince of the power of the air;12 and, it is very evident that they possess a power that none but those who have the Priesthood can control, as we have before adverted to, in the case of the sons of Sceva.13

Having said so much upon the general principles, without referring to the peculiar situation, power, and influence of the magicians of Egypt,14 the wizards15 and witches16 of the Jews, the oracles of the heathen, their necromancers,17 soothsayers,18 and astrologers,19 the maniacs or those possessed of devils in the Apostles’ days,20 we will notice, and try to detect (so far as we have the Scriptures for our aid) some few instances of the development of false spirits in more modern times, and in this our day.21
The “French Prophets” were possessed of a spirit that deceived; they existed in Vivaris and Dauphany, in great num-
